I'm not saying it's right or wrong, this is just the style we have through out the codebase. We can start to change it, but I would rather start that as a separate effort.

Here are some samples of the pattern already in use:
https://github.com/onap/so/blob/40396c8763a600059d025fb202802ac70d406b08/adapters/mso-openstack-adapters/src/test/java/org/onap/so/adapters/tenant/MsoTenantAdapterImplTest.java#L41

Hi Max,
I agree that we should follow one style in tests, using Mockito annotations like @InjectMocks. No problem with that. 
But my comment is for @Spy anno in this particular test class, which means we declare tested object as real object and mock in the same time.
In these tests I do not need to declare spy object, this anno is useless and misleading for code readers.

Generally I think that @Spy should be used carefully, only in particular cases where it is really needed.

I saw that in some tests there is @Spy anno although it is useless, and it is wrong. Do not put anno that you are not using.

All I want is to remove this one anno - @Spy, from this class. All tests pass because tested object does act as mock in any of these tests. The rest of changes that you made are ok.
